name: Email containing Sensitive File Sent to Potentially Hostile Host
notes:
This rule triggers when an email that contains a sensitive file being sent to a host that is known for hostile activities, such as Phishing, Spam, Malware, Botnet Command and Control, or Cryptocurrency Mining. The Files in Sensitive Directories reference set is populated by the Files in Sensitive File Directories rule.

Note: The Sensitive Directories reference set must be populated.
bb_references: BB:Exfiltration: Files in Sensitive Directories match at least 1 times with the same Message ID (custom) in 5 minutes after BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Email Host match
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
when BB:Exfiltration: Files in Sensitive Directories match at least 1 times with the same Message ID (custom) in 5 minutes after BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Email Host match
thresholds: when BB:Exfiltration: Files in Sensitive Directories match at least 1 times with the same Message ID (custom) in 5 minutes after BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Email Host match
TD_list: APPLY Email containing Sensitive File Sent to Potentially Hostile Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
AND when BB:Exfiltration: Files in Sensitive Directories match at least 1 times with the same Message ID (custom) in 5 minutes after BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Email Host match
RED_list: Event name: Email containing Sensitive File Sent to Potentially Hostile Host
Event description: Detects when an email that contains a sensitive file is sent to a host that is known for hostile activities.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Recipients (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
